root_text.sn55.3:1.1: Ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā rājagahe viharati veḷuvane kalandakanivāpe.
root_text.sn55.3:1.2: Tena kho pana samayena dīghāvu upāsako ābādhiko hoti dukkhito bāḷhagilāno.
root_text.sn55.3:1.3: Atha kho dīghāvu upāsako pitaraṁ jotikaṁ gahapatiṁ āmantesi:
root_text.sn55.3:1.4: “ehi tvaṁ, gahapati, yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkama; upasaṅkamitvā mama vacanena bhagavato pāde sirasā vanda:
root_text.sn55.3:1.5: ‘dīghāvu, bhante, upāsako ābādhiko hoti dukkhito bāḷhagilāno.
root_text.sn55.3:1.8: ‘sādhu kira, bhante, bhagavā yena dīghāvussa upāsakassa nivesanaṁ tenupasaṅkamatu anukampaṁ upādāyā’”ti.
root_text.sn55.3:1.9: “Evaṁ, tātā”ti kho jotiko gahapati dīghāvussa upāsakassa paṭissutvā yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi. Ekamantaṁ nisinno kho jotiko gahapati bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn55.3:1.10: “dīghāvu, bhante, upāsako ābādhiko hoti dukkhito bāḷhagilāno.
root_text.sn55.3:1.13: ‘sādhu kira, bhante, bhagavā yena dīghāvussa upāsakassa nivesanaṁ tenupasaṅkamatu anukampaṁ upādāyā’”ti.
root_text.sn55.3:2.1: Atha kho bhagavā nivāsetvā pattacīvaramādāya yena dīghāvussa upāsakassa nivesanaṁ tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā paññatte āsane nisīdi. Nisajja kho bhagavā dīghāvuṁ upāsakaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn55.3:2.2: “kacci te, dīghāvu, khamanīyaṁ, kacci yāpanīyaṁ? Kacci dukkhā vedanā paṭikkamanti, no abhikkamanti; paṭikkamosānaṁ paññāyati, no abhikkamo”ti?
root_text.sn55.3:2.3: “Na me, bhante, khamanīyaṁ, na yāpanīyaṁ. Bāḷhā me dukkhā vedanā abhikkamanti, no paṭikkamanti; abhikkamosānaṁ paññāyati, no paṭikkamo”ti.
root_text.sn55.3:2.5: ‘buddhe aveccappasādena samannāgato bhavissāmi—itipi so bhagavā arahaṁ sammāsambuddho vijjācaraṇasampanno sugato lokavidū anuttaro purisadammasārathi satthā devamanussānaṁ buddho bhagavāti.
root_text.sn55.3:2.8: ariyakantehi sīlehi samannāgato bhavissāmi akhaṇḍehi …pe… samādhisaṁvattanikehi’.
root_text.sn55.3:3.1: “Yānimāni, bhante, bhagavatā cattāri sotāpattiyaṅgāni desitāni, saṁvijjante dhammā mayi, ahañca tesu dhammesu sandissāmi.
root_text.sn55.3:3.2: Ahañhi, bhante, buddhe aveccappasādena samannāgato—itipi so bhagavā …pe… satthā devamanussānaṁ buddho bhagavāti.
root_text.sn55.3:3.6: “Tasmātiha tvaṁ, dīghāvu, imesu catūsu sotāpattiyaṅgesu patiṭṭhāya cha vijjābhāgiye dhamme uttari bhāveyyāsi.
root_text.sn55.3:3.7: Idha tvaṁ, dīghāvu, sabbasaṅkhāresu aniccānupassī viharāhi, anicce dukkhasaññī, dukkhe anattasaññī pahānasaññī virāgasaññī nirodhasaññīti.
root_text.sn55.3:4.1: “Yeme, bhante, bhagavatā cha vijjābhāgiyā dhammā desitā, saṁvijjante dhammā mayi, ahañca tesu dhammesu sandissāmi.
root_text.sn55.3:4.2: Ahañhi, bhante, sabbasaṅkhāresu aniccānupassī viharāmi, anicce dukkhasaññī, dukkhe anattasaññī pahānasaññī virāgasaññī nirodhasaññī.
root_text.sn55.3:4.3: Api ca me, bhante, evaṁ hoti:
root_text.sn55.3:4.4: ‘mā hevāyaṁ jotiko gahapati mamaccayena vighātaṁ āpajjī’”ti.
root_text.sn55.3:4.6: Iṅgha tvaṁ, tāta dīghāvu, yadeva te bhagavā āha, tadeva tvaṁ sādhukaṁ manasi karohī”ti.
root_text.sn55.3:5.1: Atha kho bhagavā dīghāvuṁ upāsakaṁ iminā ovādena ovaditvā uṭṭhāyāsanā pakkāmi.
root_text.sn55.3:5.2: Atha kho dīghāvu upāsako acirapakkantassa bhagavato kālamakāsi.
root_text.sn55.3:5.3: Atha kho sambahulā bhikkhū yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkamiṁsu; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdiṁsu. Ekamantaṁ nisinnā kho te bhikkhū bhagavantaṁ etadavocuṁ:
root_text.sn55.3:5.4: “yo so, bhante, dīghāvu nāma upāsako bhagavatā saṅkhittena ovādena ovadito so kālaṅkato.
root_text.sn55.3:5.6: “Paṇḍito, bhikkhave, dīghāvu upāsako, paccapādi dhammassānudhammaṁ, na ca maṁ dhammādhikaraṇaṁ vihesesi.
root_text.sn55.3:5.7: Dīghāvu, bhikkhave, upāsako pañcannaṁ orambhāgiyānaṁ saṁyojanānaṁ parikkhayā opapātiko tattha parinibbāyī anāvattidhammo tasmā lokā”ti.

translation_text.sn55.3:1.1: At one time the Buddha was staying near Rājagaha, in the Bamboo Grove, the squirrels’ feeding ground.
translation_text.sn55.3:1.2: Now at that time the lay follower Dīghāvu was sick, suffering, gravely ill.
translation_text.sn55.3:1.3: Then he addressed his father, the householder Jotika,
translation_text.sn55.3:1.4: “Please, householder, go to the Buddha, and in my name bow with your head to his feet. Say to him:
translation_text.sn55.3:1.5: ‘Sir, the lay follower Dīghāvu is sick, suffering, gravely ill.
translation_text.sn55.3:1.6: He bows with his head to your feet.’
translation_text.sn55.3:1.8: ‘Sir, please visit him at his home out of sympathy.’”
translation_text.sn55.3:1.9: “Yes, dear,” replied Jotika. He did as Dīghāvu asked.
translation_text.sn55.3:2.1: Then the Buddha robed up in the morning and, taking his bowl and robe, went to the home of the lay follower Dīghāvu, sat down on the seat spread out, and said to him,
translation_text.sn55.3:2.2: “I hope you’re keeping well, Dīghāvu; I hope you’re all right. I hope that your pain is fading, not growing, that its fading is evident, not its growing.”
translation_text.sn55.3:2.3: “Sir, I’m not keeping well, I’m not getting by. The pain is terrible and growing, not fading; its growing is evident, not its fading.”
translation_text.sn55.3:2.4: “So, Dīghāvu, you should train like this:
translation_text.sn55.3:2.5: ‘I will have experiential confidence in the Buddha …
translation_text.sn55.3:2.8: And I will have the ethical conduct loved by the noble ones … leading to immersion.’
translation_text.sn55.3:3.1: “Sir, these four factors of stream-entry that were taught by the Buddha are found in me, and I exhibit them.
translation_text.sn55.3:3.2: For I have experiential confidence in the Buddha …
translation_text.sn55.3:3.5: And I have the ethical conduct loved by the noble ones … leading to immersion.”
translation_text.sn55.3:3.6: “In that case, Dīghāvu, grounded on these four factors of stream-entry you should further develop these six things that play a part in realization.
translation_text.sn55.3:3.7: You should meditate observing the impermanence of all conditions, perceiving suffering in impermanence, perceiving not-self in suffering, perceiving giving up, perceiving fading away, and perceiving cessation.
translation_text.sn55.3:4.1: “These six things that play a part in realization that were taught by the Buddha are found in me, and I exhibit them.
translation_text.sn55.3:4.2: For I meditate observing the impermanence of all conditions, perceiving suffering in impermanence, perceiving not-self in suffering, perceiving giving up, perceiving fading away, and perceiving cessation.
translation_text.sn55.3:4.4: ‘I hope Jotika doesn’t suffer distress when I’ve gone.’”
translation_text.sn55.3:4.5: Jotika said, “Dear Dīghāvu, don’t focus on that.
translation_text.sn55.3:4.6: Come on, dear Dīghāvu, you should closely focus on what the Buddha is saying.”
translation_text.sn55.3:5.1: When the Buddha had given this advice he got up from his seat and left.
translation_text.sn55.3:5.2: Not long after the Buddha left, Dīghāvu passed away.
translation_text.sn55.3:5.3: Then several mendicants went up to the Buddha, bowed, sat down to one side, and said to him:
translation_text.sn55.3:5.4: “Sir, the lay follower named Dīghāvu, who was advised in brief by the Buddha, has passed away.
translation_text.sn55.3:5.5: Where has he been reborn in his next life?”
translation_text.sn55.3:5.6: “Mendicants, the lay follower Dīghāvu was astute. He practiced in line with the teaching, and did not trouble me about the teachings.
translation_text.sn55.3:5.7: With the ending of the five lower fetters, he’s been reborn spontaneously, and will become extinguished there, not liable to return from that world.”

variant_text.sn55.3:5.6: upāsako, paccapādi → ahosi paccapādi (bj, pts1ed); ahosi saccavādī (sya-all, km, mr) | ca maṁ dhammādhikaraṇaṁ → na ca dhammādhikaraṇaṁ (sya-all, km, pts1ed, mr) | vihesesi → viheṭhesi (itipi aññattha)
